meaning: n.大会,审议会adj.未决议的,无实际意义的vt.提出...供讨论
examples: He argued that the issue had become moot since the board had changed its policy.
For Layton, the question was moot right now.
How long he'll be able to do so is a moot point.
One can easily debate the relative merits of either path, but the point is largely moot.
Whether encouraging the abstemious to drink wine would cause them to lead healthier lives is moot.
